element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns fuel management, speed control, radar reading, and landing mechanics before combat pressure
Primary weapon available immediately with no ammo scarcity
Player must choose between quantity (40 small), power (10 large), or balance (20 medium) before takeoff
Navigation and targeting introduced via dual radar system (Wide Area and Immediate Range)
Danger lamp warning system cues player; chaff grenades available as countermeasure
Requires hovering, sight alignment, descent timing; failure allows retry without penalty

element_introduction_order.first_appears: After helicopter lands at enemy heliport
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Auto-scrolling right; player shoots enemies and oil drums; ammo and health management begins
Machine gun soldiers (2 HP in US version), positioned at varying screen heights
Resource scarcity introduced; player must manage ammunition carefully in crowded situations
Interior base navigation; collecting vital items and avoiding traps
Obstacle avoidance mechanic in confined spaces

typical_relief_triggers: Landing at allied heliport for repair/refuel
Exiting base and escaping to helicopter
typical_tension_triggers: Crowded enemy soldier waves in gun shooting mode
Navigating maze traps with low health
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Multi-phase tension cycling: high-pressure helicopter dogfighting → moderate-tension auto-scroll shooting → sustained low-tension maze exploration, repeating across four missions with escalating difficulty

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Allied heliports offer optional equipment upgrades and repairs; player can strategically choose between fuel, chaff grenades, or ground missiles
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Oil drums in gun shooting segments eliminate all on-screen enemies when destroyed; weapon crates contain hidden pickups

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like helicopter controls introduced at mission start with minimal initial threats; fuel and speed management prioritized over combat
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Enemy soldiers become more numerous in gun shooting segments; ammunition scarcity increases (US version has tougher enemies: 2 HP machine gunners, 5 HP turrets); missile management becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Mission 3 takes place entirely in air (pure helicopter combat); maze exploration becomes more complex; combined threat of fuel, damage, and time pressure
notable_spikes: Transition from helicopter to gun shooting mode requires shift in input method and pacing
Crowded enemy waves in late gun shooting segments requiring careful ammunition management
Mission 3 removal of ground-based safety valve (no heliport landings)

notable_level_patterns.description: Helicopter approach → land at enemy heliport → gun shooting corridor → maze base infiltration → escape sequence
Mission 3 eliminates ground segments; player engages entirely in helicopter dogfighting with extended fuel management and enemy interception
Allied heliports serve as safe zones for repair/refuel and equipment reconfiguration; enemy heliports are forced landing destinations for objective infiltration
